creator: mandree@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi Gustau,

I hope you're doing fine.

A couple of weeks ago you rejected a FreeBSD port upgrade submitted by
Barbara (cc:d) and stated you wanted to do your own upgrade to a newer
0.3.X release.  I cannot seem to find a relevant PR, or update.

The old one: <http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/155563>.

Were there any particular concerns with this PR, technical ones, known
issues, that should make us wait for 0.3.11 or 0.4?

What's your status on this?

NOTE: I am somewhat inclined to merge Barbara's submission into the port
barring any technical objections or a superseding PR on your part so
that the ports tree remains in good shape.


If you're out of time, we have several options to deal with that
situation and help you out -- all are form-free, just two lines of email
as a bug followup suffice:

- You can authorize any particular committer, or all, (where I'd be the
primary suspect) to deal with this one (and only one) update --
personally, I'd make sure the port builds, doesn't revert past fixes,
and passes trivial tests, and if possible, together with Barbara (the
submitter and thus a known interested user of the port).

- OR You can authorize other parties you trust to co-maintain the port
(this is informal and recorded in a comment in the Makefile, as in, for
instance, databases/db47)

- OR You can ask that we find another maintainer, temporarily or permanently

- OR, if you repeatedly cannot deal with PRs in time, the committers can
also override your maintainership and commit without your consent, as
suggested above.


Please respond ASAP.

Thank you, gracias y obrigado.

Best regards,
Matthias
